title: Building My Personal Homepage from Scratch
excerpt: A deep dive into how I designed and built a lightweight personal homepage system using PHP and JSON storage.
content:
Introduction

I've always wanted a digital space that's truly mine. So I spent a few days building this personal homepage system from the ground up.

Key Features

  • Frontend built with Tailwind CSS + vanilla JavaScript for responsiveness and dark mode
  • Backend uses PHP + JSON file storage - simple and lightweight, no database needed
  • Rich text diary entries with image support
  • Guestbook with nested replies and likes

The whole experience taught me a lot about keeping things simple yet functional.

title: Travel Photography: Slow Living in Yunnan
excerpt: Rented a small courtyard by the Erhai Lake and stayed for two weeks. A journey of self-reflection and beautiful landscapes.
content:
The Charm of Dali

Traveling south from the ancient city of Lijiang, I arrived in Dali. The Cangshan Mountains and Erhai Lake under blue skies truly lived up to their reputation.

Here, I completely slowed down. Mornings coding at a cafe in the old town, afternoons exploring nearby villages on an e-bike.

Photography Highlights

Brought both film and digital cameras, shot nearly 30 rolls of film. Especially loved using black and white film to capture portraits of local elders.

title: Thoughts on Productivity and Creativity
excerpt: After reading 'Deep Work' and 'Atomic Habits', I've been experimenting with methods to maintain high creativity and efficiency.
content:
The Importance of Deep Work

In the age of information overload, staying focused is increasingly difficult. I tried the 'time blocking' method: dedicating 3-4 hours each day to deep work.

My Practice

  1. Golden 2 Hours in the Morning: No phone after waking up, jump straight into the most important task.
  2. Pomodoro + Walking: Work for 50 minutes, mandatory 10-minute break with a walk.

These simple changes have dramatically improved my output quality.
